calendar.title: Pacific Fusion Internal Contract Review #1
calendar.prep_context: Swan reviewing A133 + Exhibit A today. PF set 7/14 hard deadline for A133/A102/GMP1 execution. Zurich pulled out of PF insurance quoting — needs alternate carrier. Key question for review: what open commercial/legal items must resolve before 7/14?

bucket1.subject: PF/Clayco - Preliminary Contract Comments
bucket1.body_preview: All, PF is tracking toward these dates for contract execution: Executed by 7/14 — A133 Master Agreement, A102 General Conditions, GMP 1. GMP 2 - Executed by 10/9. GMP 3 - Executed by 9/22. GMP 4 - Executed by 11/20.
bucket1.full_thread_content: Courtney (PF): All, PF is tracking toward these dates for contract execution: Executed by 7/14 — A133 Master Agreement, A102 General Conditions, GMP 1. GMP 2 - Executed by 10/9. GMP 3 - Executed by 9/22. GMP 4 - Executed by 11/20. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Ryan (sent): Updated team on PF contract comment timing. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Abbott (Clayco): A133 contract review status provided to team.
bucket1.sent_body: Team — updated everyone on PF contract comment timing and review status.

extracted.ai_summary: Pacific Fusion set hard execution deadlines: A133/A102/GMP1 by July 14, GMP2 by Oct 9, GMP3 by Sept 22, GMP4 by Nov 20. Internal contract review #1 is today at 1 PM MST with Swan and Tighe.

action_items.text: Attend PF Internal Contract Review #1 today at 1 PM MST — be prepared on A133 + Exhibit A status; flag Zurich insurance gap to Swan
Resolve all open Clayco contract comments before 7/14 — escalate blockers immediately after today's review

commitments.text: Swan reviewing A133 + Exhibit A today, A201 over the weekend

pending_decisions.question: What Clayco commercial or legal issues remain open on A133/A102 that could block 7/14 execution?
pending_decisions.context: PF has set a hard deadline. Swan reviewing today. Ryan needs to come to the 1 PM meeting knowing Clayco's open items and which ones are negotiable vs. firm.

pending_decisions.question: GMP3 (9/22) executes before GMP2 (10/9) — is this intentional PF sequencing or a typo?
pending_decisions.context: Out-of-sequence GMP execution dates in Courtney's email. Should be flagged and confirmed in today's review.

risk_signals.signal: Hard 7/14 deadline for A133/GMP1 — any open legal/commercial issues must resolve in <18 days with legal counsel on both sides
GMP3 (9/22) executes before GMP2 (10/9) in PF's schedule — sequencing anomaly to confirm
Greenberg Traurig on PF side — owner has experienced construction counsel in the room

decisions_made.decision: Pacific Fusion has set GMP execution schedule across 4 GMPs spanning July-November 2026

key_facts.fact: A133 + A102 + GMP1 execution deadline: July 14, 2026
GMP2 deadline: October 9, 2026; GMP3: September 22, 2026; GMP4: November 20, 2026
PF legal counsel: Greenberg Traurig (Eric Hieber, Jensen D)
Clayco legal: Catherine Swan (Senior Associate Counsel, CO + UT bars)

bucket1.subject: RE: Project Bravo deck updates, and content needs

bucket1.full_thread_content: Scott Watkins: Ryan, Thoughts on generating a Project profile for Pacific Fusion? We could make it a confidential client nuclear fusion profile. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Ryan (sent): Will work on it today, need to review NDA first. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- [Earlier: Bravo deck coordination thread with Kopp, Tighe, Dziekan, Randolph, Baldock, Lechter, Cross, Person, Young, Schnur]
bucket1.sent_body: Will work on it today, need to review NDA first.
bucket1.thread_subject: Project Bravo deck updates, and content needs

extracted.ai_summary: Scott Watkins (SVP Industrial) is asking Ryan to consider creating a confidential PF project profile for the Bravo pursuit deck. Ryan committed to working on it today pending NDA review.

action_items.text: Review PF NDA to determine what can be disclosed in a project profile for external use, then respond to Watkins with decision
Create or confirm direction on Pacific Fusion confidential project profile for Bravo deck

commitments.text: Ryan committed to working on Bravo deck today pending NDA review

pending_decisions.question: Can Pacific Fusion be referenced (even as 'confidential client') in the Bravo pursuit deck, given NDA constraints?
pending_decisions.context: Watkins proposed a nuclear fusion confidential profile. Ryan flagged NDA as a gating issue. Decision needs to be made today since deck work is in progress.

risk_signals.signal: NDA may restrict PF disclosure — using PF without confirming NDA scope is a contractual risk

key_facts.fact: Bravo pursuit deck is an active effort with 13 internal participants including Kopp, Tighe, Dziekan, Lechter (LJC)

bucket3.subject: Project Solis - Foundation selection / Building Reduction
bucket3.body_preview: TT Team - do you have updated CAD files with the removal of the area between grid 10-12? We'd like to pick that up in our design to avoid rework.
bucket3.full_thread_content: Craig Streit (WGI): TT Team - do you have updated CAD files with the removal of the area between grid 10-12? We'd like to pick that up in our design to avoid rework. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Koenigs (TT): Craig - With the higher stiffness of the rigid inclusions, are there other items that we need to be aware of as we design the foundations? Specifically, do the rigid inclusions perform as point supports such that we would need to design the mat to span between them? ---MESSAGE BREAK--- [Earlier messages in foundation selection thread]
bucket3.sent_body: Not yet, likely middle of July. [Ryan to Bill Huie on foundation selection timeline]
bucket3.thread_subject: Project Solis - Foundation selection / Building Reduction

extracted.ai_summary: Active Solis foundation engineering coordination: WGI needs updated CAD from TT reflecting grid 10-12 building reduction, and TT is asking WGI whether rigid inclusions act as point supports requiring mat to span (structural/cost implication).

action_items.text: TT to provide WGI updated CAD files reflecting grid 10-12 area removal

pending_decisions.question: Do rigid inclusions act as point supports requiring mat foundation to span between them — what is WGI's answer?
pending_decisions.context: TT (Koenigs) asked this of WGI (Craig). If yes, mat design becomes more complex and costly. Ryan should monitor for WGI's response.

risk_signals.signal: Rigid inclusions as point supports = more complex mat design = potential cost increase vs. current estimate
WGI rework risk if TT delays updated CAD files for grid 10-12 reduction
Building reduction (grid 10-12) is a scope change — should be tracked for contract and cost impact

key_facts.fact: WGI specs: 750 psi, rigid inclusions ~5ft edge-to-edge
Building reduction: area between grid 10-12 being removed from Solis program
Ryan told Bill Huie foundation selection is not yet decided, likely middle of July

bucket3.subject: Pacific Fusion - Fill Material
bucket3.body_preview: We do not anticipate any changes at this point and will have the same recommendations in the sealed version.
bucket3.full_thread_content: Matt Cramer (Geomat): We do not anticipate any changes at this point and will have the same recommendations in the sealed version. To simplify, based on the laboratory testing from the borings, it looks like most everything that was tested and classified as [fill material] is usable.

extracted.ai_summary: Geomat confirms no changes expected in sealed fill material report — lab testing from borings supports existing recommendations, material is usable.

decisions_made.decision: Fill material classification confirmed as usable — sealed Geomat report will match preliminary with no changes

key_facts.fact: Geomat sealed report: same fill material recommendations as preliminary, no changes expected

bucket3.subject: RE: Pacific Fusion Call
bucket3.body_preview: I just heard back from our East region. Unfortunately, they have decided to not continue in quoting and have shut down their underwriting for this project.
bucket3.full_thread_content: Caleb McIntosh (Zurich): I just heard back from our East region. Unfortunately, they have decided to not continue in quoting and have shut down their underwriting for this project. So we won't need to have a call at this juncture. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Nocona Schulz: Thanks for the update, Caleb! It gives you more time to get my policies cranked out. ---MESSAGE BREAK--- Ryan (sent): Available for AM call.
bucket3.sent_body: Available for AM call re Pacific Fusion insurance.

extracted.ai_summary: Zurich's East region pulled out of quoting Pacific Fusion insurance — project closed for them, no call needed. Schulz (SVP Insurance) is managing the carrier track.

risk_signals.signal: Zurich pulled out of PF insurance underwriting — carrier gap exists, Schulz needs alternate carrier for PF

decisions_made.decision: Zurich East declined to quote PF insurance — thread closed

key_facts.fact: Zurich not providing insurance for Pacific Fusion — East region shut down underwriting
Nocona Schulz (Clayco SVP Corporate Insurance) is the internal contact managing PF insurance procurement
